reviews.review_description: If you like fighting games, this is a good one to have. Easy to pick up but still challenging enough to keep you hooked.

reviews.review_title: Its a Good buy for this price
reviews.review_description:
I did not expect this much for this price,it has a good online and offline boxing game with few MMA moves and has multiple mini games and the best one is the Fitness zone where you hit the targets and burn calories.
Although expecting the future updates will have the more polished version.

currently unplayable for me, the hands are positioned upwards and I feel nauseous when the character moves on its own with the kicks. On the other hand, at this price, it's hard to complain, the mini games are fun, I'll come back to them after a few updates

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback we really appreciate it.
The hand positioning can be adjusted using the in-game calibration, this should help better align the hands with your natural stance and improve overall comfort. We recommend giving that a try based on your setup.
We’re also actively working on improving tracking accuracy and reducing discomfort during movement to make the experience smoother in upcoming updates.

Glad you’re enjoying the mini games more updates on the way.

reviews.review_title: Great mix of fun and fitness
reviews.review_description: Didn’t expect to sweat this much 😅 It’s actually a solid workout while still feeling like a proper game.
I enjoyed the game and i think its a decent 4 star game in the store and 1 additional star it deserves for its mini games and music punching mode.

The game delivers intense, immersive cage fights that truly make you feel like a professional fighter. The striking feels powerful, movement is smooth, and the overall atmosphere of the arena is highly realistic. Training modes and multiplayer add great replay value, and it also gives a solid physical workout. Despite being VR, the combat feels engaging and exciting from start to finish.
I have been playing it for a while now and i can say its quite a good game

As a real MMA fan, this scratches that itch perfectly. Love being able to practice different fighting styles and the submission mechanics are surprisingly well done. The multiplayer community is growing which is awesome. Highly recommend!
i have no idea how to get in the cage it's just putting me in practice i need help

MMA Cage Conquest delivers an intense and highly immersive VR combat experience that truly makes you feel like you’re inside a real MMA cage. From the moment the match starts, the atmosphere, physics, and responsiveness pull you straight into the fight.

The single-player mode is perfect for training and skill-building. The AI opponents are challenging and reactive, pushing you to improve your timing, defense, and striking techniques. Every punch feels powerful and satisfying, making workouts feel fun and rewarding rather than repetitive.

The multiplayer mode is where the game truly shines. Facing real players adds a whole new level of excitement and unpredictability. The smooth networking and accurate motion tracking make online fights feel fair, competitive, and adrenaline-packed. Winning a match after a tough exchange feels incredibly rewarding.

The boxing mechanics are well-designed, with realistic movement, solid hit detection, and fluid animations. The cage environment, sound effects, and crowd presence add to the realism, making every fight feel like a high-stakes championship bout.

Overall, MMA Cage Conquest is a must-play for VR boxing and MMA fans. Whether you’re looking for an intense workout, competitive multiplayer action, or immersive single-player battles, this game delivers on all fronts. Highly recommended for anyone who wants a true VR combat experience. 🥊🔥

reviews.review_title: Finally,a VR MMA game that gets it right
reviews.review_description: The training modes are actually useful for improving your technique, and the Power Punch mini-game is addictive. The online multiplayer is where this really shines - nothing beats the rush of landing a knockout against a real opponent. Worth every penny at $7.99.

This delivers an incredible VR experience for MMA fans. The game features intuitive controls, realistic fight mechanics, and stunning visuals, making you feel like you're truly in the cage. The sound design enhances the immersion, with lifelike crowd noises and impactful combat sounds. Customization and progression are satisfying, allowing you to develop your own fighter and rise through the ranks. Overall, MMA Cage Conquest is a five-star VR game that sets a new standard.
Punches feel solid, movement is smooth, and the game atmosphere really pulls you in. It’s a legit workout and stays exciting even after multiple fights.

It has been a solid surprise. The fitness zone actually gets you moving, though it could use more music and a couple extra maps. Multiplayer fights hit hard and feel intense with online players, while the offline AI is great and hard when you just want to practice.

MMA Cage Conquest has evolved significantly since its initial release, and I truly appreciate the improvements the developers have made. The enhancements to graphics and gameplay systems show genuine effort and dedication.

However, despite having "MMA" in the title, the game currently only offers kickboxing gameplay. The grappling elements that are essential to MMA are still absent, which is disappointing for fans of the sport.

I understand that fully recreating grappling in VR is extremely challenging from a technical standpoint. That's why I'd like to suggest a hybrid approach: when players clinch or go to the ground, the game could switch to a command-input system similar to traditional fighting games, allowing players to choose their responses and techniques.

If this type of grappling system were implemented, this game could become a revolutionary tool for learning MMA at home and would truly transform the VR fighting game genre. I'm hopeful for future updates that bring us closer to a complete MMA experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the thoughtful and constructive feedback,we’re glad you’ve noticed the improvements to graphics and gameplay. Our team is constantly working on updates, polishing the overall experience, improving visuals, and adding new features with each iteration.

You’re absolutely right the striking is strong,and grappling in VR is a major technical challenge. However, it’s on our development pipeline, and we’re steadily working toward it to ensure we deliver the best possible experience for our players. Your idea of a hybrid, command-based system is a compelling approach and aligns with discussions we’ve had internally.

Thanks again for your support,your realism about the challenges, and your passion for the sport. We’re excited to keep building—and feedback like yours genuinely helps shape the future of the game.

reviews.review_title: MMA VR: Intense Fights, Real Action
reviews.review_description: This MMA VR game is a blast! The fights feel real, with powerful strikes and smooth moves. The arena’s atmosphere pulls you right in, and the training and multiplayer keep it fresh. Plus, it's a solid workout. It’s pure excitement from start to finish.
Every hit has impact, movement and fights stay tense from start to finish. Simple to pick up, but winning still takes skill and good timing.

reviews.review_title: Its a good buy for this price
reviews.review_description: I have not seen the AI so strong that it took me 3 matches to beat this character once.Visuals could be better though.overall i liked it along with fitness zone like a mini beat saberr 😅

Every match feels different because you’re fighting real people, not predictable AI. Dodging punches in real-time gets your heart racing fast. It’s competitive, chaotic, and super satisfying when you win. Totally worth the price.

reviews.review_title: Didn’t expect to sweat this much in VR
reviews.review_description: This game is no joke. The drills and combos actually make you move like you’re in a real gym session. After 20 minutes I’m drenched. Way more fun than traditional workouts. Easily one of the best fitness games I’ve bought.
I’ve played a few matches and it genuinely feels intense inside the cage. Multiplayer is fun, though punches don’t always register perfectly.

reviews.review_title: The Champion's Journey mode is engaging
reviews.review_description: the progression system keeps you coming back. Graphics are pretty good for the price point. My only complaint is I wish there were more arenas to unlock. Still, highly recommend for MMA fans.
this game is bad i want to refund

reviews.review_title: This game gives me an incredible workout
reviews.review_description: I've been using the Punch Beats mode almost daily and I'm actually seeing results. The leaderboards keep me motivated to push harder. If you're looking for fitness and fun combined, this is it.
The Punch Puppet mode really helps with timing and reflexes. I've gotten way better at the actual fights from just doing the training mini-games. Perfect mix of workout and entertainment.
The online championships are competitive and fair. Climbing the leaderboards is addictive. The customization options for your fighter are nice too. This is what VR sports games should be.

reviews.review_title: I love how beginner-friendly it is
reviews.review_description: The tutorials really break things down and help you learn proper technique instead of just button mashing. Within a week I felt faster and more confident. Perfect mix of game + training.

reviews.review_title: Really enjoying this as new to Vr games
reviews.review_description: The tutorial is helpful and the difficulty ramps up nicely. Online matches can be tough but that just makes you want to train more. Great value for money.
Put some hours into this and it delivers a solid VR fighting experience. It’s fun, tiring in a good way, and easy to jump into.

reviews.review_title: Not gonna Lie - Great app for workout
reviews.review_description:
I mean its not perfect but the gameplay and multiplayer feels good and real but it needs some improvement in visuals.
 
The best part is the fitness zone which makes you feel its worth every penny you spent.
The VR movement feels natural, punches land with impact, and  atmosphere feels you like you are really inside the fighting arena. Great fun for MMA and fighting fan!

reviews.review_title: Fun, intense, and a real workout
reviews.review_description: Fast, realistic fights that keep you sweating and coming back for more, and with the addition of Fitness Mode, the gameplay and fitness combo is an excellent value at the current price.
Amazing game worth playing.
In Love with the fitness boxing mode and the mma multiplayer is no less,played a match with a friend to show him who is the boss 😅😅

It can be used for daily cardio like a gym without monthly subscription 😄 Big thumbs up to the devs
Started playing just to try it out but ended up spending hours on it. The fights feel engaging and not repetitive.

I originally left one of the more positive early reviews for this game (almost a year ago).  It wasn't great, but I play it every now and then, and could see potential here.  Since then though, this game has supposedly had significant updates at least twice.  However, this game has never updated once for me.  Still the same 4 original fighters, and 2 rings, and not much else.  Nothing added, and nothing fixed.  I've manually searched for updates, and there's nothing showing.   Have  uninstalled, and reinstalled, nothing.  No idea what's going on with it...

developer_response.body:
Hi there! Thanks so much for your early support and for sticking with the game. We’re sorry to hear you haven’t seen any of the updates — we’ve actually released several major updates since launch, including new fighters, more rings, improved gameplay, and various bug fixes.

Please double-check that you're running the latest version of the game. If updates still aren’t showing, try reinstalling again and make sure your app store isn’t holding back the latest version.

Also, we’d love to help you directly — feel free to join our official Discord server here: 
+https://discord.gg/qyNj972zqM
 Our team and community are active there and can assist you with any issues.

Thanks again for your feedback — we really appreciate it! 🙌

developer_response.body:
Hi there, we're sorry to hear that the game didn’t meet your expectations. We understand how frustrating it can be when a game doesn’t match what you hoped for.

We're continuously working on improving the graphics, gameplay, and overall experience based on player feedback. Advertisements are meant to capture attention, but we never want them to feel misleading — your feedback helps us do better.

If you're open to it, we'd love to hear more details about what specifically disappointed you so we can keep improving. You're also welcome to join our Discord at: 
https://discord.gg/qyNj972zqM    to share your thoughts directly with the team.

Thanks for giving the game a try.

Hi there, we're sorry to hear that you're disappointed with the game. We appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback — it's really helpful for us as we continue to improve.

We’re actively working on upgrading the graphics, adding new combat mechanics, and improving hit reactions to make the gameplay feel more realistic and satisfying. These kinds of updates take time, but we’re committed to making the game better with every release.

reviews.review_description: never wanted to die so much can’t belive i wasted my money on this let my gran play and she flung herself down the stairs eat ur money instead

There’s no tutorial, doesn’t appear to be a way for you to do knees or kicks or any grappling. It’s basically boxing with your opponent doing kick boxing. The striking isn’t 100%accurate. Also seems to glitch after you finish a fight cause your viewpoint becomes 1 foot off the gym floor you started in and you can’t do anything to fix it besides restart the game. The concept of a true mma game has a lot of potential but this isn’t anywhere close to it

I’ve had the game for several months and keep waiting for an update, but it simply doesn’t work. The punches are inaccurate and there is no tutorial that teaches you how to play the game. I only gave it two stars because I like the concept but the game is far from playable
￼

developer_response.body:
Hi,
We’re really sorry to have kept you waiting and appreciate your patience. Our team has been working hard behind the scenes, carefully listening to player feedback like yours. We’re excited to share that we’ve recently updated the game with major improvements for new players.

We’d love for you to give it another try and let us know how it feels now. Your feedback truly helps us make the experience better.

Thank you again for supporting the concept—we’re committed to making it as enjoyable and polished as possible.

— The MMA Cage Conquest Team.

reviews.review_description: Visuals are terrible and jumpy in play. Made me feel sick. Can't knee or kick or takedown. Or if you can I've got no idea how. Need to move away from arcade style and more towards realistic game play. Lots of potential but at the moment it's terrible. Sorry. Unplayable.

reviews.review_title: Throw your money into the ocean instead
reviews.review_description:
This game is an insult to MMA, Bare Knuckle Boxing, and Boxing. If you want to buy this game, just give that money to a homeless person or throw it in a large body of water. I'm genuinely going to reach out to customer service and argue for a refund.

Let's start with the lies: this is not actually an MMA game at time of writing. There are no takedowns or submissions, the character I fought in the tutorial had no gloves (this game is so bad I'm refunding it not even getting through the tutorial), making this a bare knuckle boxing game. Your character does not have arms; therefore, submissions & takedowns are not possible as there is no object there to exert pressure. I mean Drunkn Bar Fight is a better MMA game as you can actually do things like a double leg takedown, guillotine chokes, arm bars even (granted that game is janky, but still). As far as boxing goes, Thrill of The Fight is far far better in every single way.

This game constantly crashes & I was unable to even play anything other than the tutorial. It's a buggy mess, and when it works the mechanics are so laughable it circles right back round to being sad. TOTF came out how long ago & this can't even open without crashing consistently?
